# terrible_community_aws_networkfirewall (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `arn` (String) The ARN of the firewall. Exactly one of *arn* or *name* must be provided.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `delete_protection` (Boolean) When *delete_protection=True*, the firewall is protected from deletion. Defaults to `false` when not provided on creation.
- `description` (String) A description for the firewall.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `name` (String) The name of the firewall. Cannot be updated after creation. Exactly one of *arn* or *name* must be provided.
- `policy` (String) The ARN of the Network Firewall policy to use for the firewall. Required when creating a new firewall.
- `policy_change_protection` (Boolean) When *policy_change_protection=True*, the firewall is protected from changes to which policy is attached to the firewall. Defaults to `false` when not provided on creation.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `purge_subnets` (Boolean) If *purge_subnets=true*, existing subnets will be removed from the firewall as necessary to match exactly what is defined by *subnets*.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Create or remove the firewall.
- `subnet_change_protection` (Boolean) When *subnet_change_protection=True*, the firewall is protected from changes to which subnets is attached to the firewall. Defaults to `false` when not provided on creation.
- `subnets` (String) The ID of the subnets to which the firewall will be associated. Required when creating a new firewall.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `wait` (Boolean) On creation, whether to wait for the firewall to reach the `READY` state. On deletion, whether to wait for the firewall to reach the `DELETED` state. On update, whether to wait for the firewall to reach the `IN_SYNC` configuration synchronization state.
- `wait_timeout` (Number) Maximum time, in seconds, to wait for the firewall to reach the expected state. Defaults to 600 seconds.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `firewall` (String) The full details of the firewall
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
